Regions Artists Timeline All works

Jan Steen

4 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
The Quack
by Jan Steen
Dutch Golden Age · c. 1650
A traveling mountebank pitches cures to a village crowd, a monkey on his shoulder. Jan Steen painted painters, drunks, taverns, and especially charlatans — the ordinary life he knew best as a tavernkeeper himself.
Esther, Ahasuerus, and Haman
by Jan Steen
Netherlands · c. 1668 · oil on canvas
The Feast of St Nicholas
by Jan Steen
Dutch Golden Age · c. 1665
A Dutch family the morning after December 5th. One daughter clutches a saint's doll and refuses to share; a boy wails because his shoe held only a birch rod. The mother conceals a smile.
The Merry Family
by Jan Steen
Dutch Golden Age · 1668
A household singing, drinking, smoking, children included — above, an inscription: "As the old sing, so pipe the young." A moralizing warning dressed as a party; Steen's signature register.
←Cristoforo Stati Paul Storr→
